language.name: Bonsai 1.7B
language.summary: Ultra-compressed 1-bit 1.7B model (248 MB). 14x smaller than FP16. Runs on virtually any device. Great for ultra-low storage or as a fast fallback.

language.name: Qwen3.5 0.8B
language.summary: Very small Qwen3.5 option for older phones and fast downloads. Good fallback when storage and memory are tight.

language.name: Bonsai 8B
language.summary: Ultra-compressed 1-bit 8B model (1.16 GB). 14x smaller than FP16 with competitive quality. Cross-platform including Android. Great when storage is tight but you need 8B-class reasoning.

language.name: Qwen3.5 2B Opus Distilled
language.summary: Distilled Qwen3.5 2B variant aimed at sharper Claude-style reasoning, coding help, and cleaner follow-through than the base 2B while staying phone-friendly.

language.name: Qwen3.5 2B
language.summary: Strong small-model upgrade over fallback tiers. This is a good default target for mainstream phones.

language.name: Hermes 3 Llama 3.2 3B
language.summary: Compact Hermes model with strong tool-calling and instruction following. Great for mobile devices. Supports structured function calling via ChatML.

language.name: Qwen3.5 4B Tuned
language.summary: Qwen3.5 4B variant tuned for more direct, less filtered replies than the base 4B while staying light enough for newer phones, tablets, and desktops.

language.name: Qwen3.5 4B
language.summary: Higher-quality Qwen3.5 option for newer phones. Likely one of the best mainstream chat models in the catalog.

language.name: Gemma 4 E2B IT
language.summary: Ultra-compact Gemma 4 with vision and 128K context. Google's smallest multimodal model — great for older phones and fast downloads.

language.name: DeepHermes 3 Llama 3 8B
language.summary: Hermes 8B with unified reasoning mode — toggle between fast responses and chain-of-thought thinking within a single model. Tool-calling included.

language.name: Hermes 3 Llama 3.1 8B
language.summary: Flagship Hermes 8B with excellent tool-calling, structured output, and multi-turn conversation. Strong all-rounder for phones and desktops.

language.name: Gemma 4 E4B IT
language.summary: Mid-range Gemma 4 with vision, 128K context, and strong multilingual support. Sweet spot for mainstream phones and tablets.

language.name: Gemma 4 E4B Tuned
language.summary: Gemma 4 E4B variant tuned for more direct, less filtered replies. Natively multimodal (text, image, video, audio) with 128K context.

language.name: Qwen3.5 9B Tuned
language.summary: Qwen3.5 9B variant tuned to feel friendlier, more direct, and less inquisitive in everyday chats. Heavy for phones and best suited to newer devices.

language.name: Qwen3.5 9B Opus Distilled
language.summary: Distilled Qwen3.5 9B variant aimed at stronger Claude-style reasoning, longer-form answers, and sharper coding help than the base 9B. Heavy for phones and a better fit for newer devices and desktops.

language.name: Qwopus3.5 9B v3
language.summary: Reasoning-enhanced Qwen3.5 9B with shorter, more stable reasoning paths. Outperforms base 9B on HumanEval (+4.87 pp) while using 25% fewer reasoning tokens.

language.name: Qwen3.5 9B Opus Distilled v2
language.summary: Improved v2 distillation of Qwen3.5 9B with 20% fewer reasoning tokens and higher accuracy. Good for offline analytical tasks, coding, and math.

language.name: Qwen3.5 9B
language.summary: Stretch goal model for flagship phones, tablets, and desktop-class devices. Expect heavier memory pressure and slower responses.

language.name: Qwen3.5 27B Opus Distilled
language.summary: Desktop-focused Qwen3.5 27B variant distilled toward Claude-style reasoning and longer-form responses. Best suited to Macs and PCs with substantial unified memory or RAM.

language.name: Qwen3.5 27B Tuned
language.summary: Desktop-focused Qwen3.5 27B variant tuned for more direct and less filtered responses than the base 27B. Best suited to Macs and PCs with substantial memory headroom.

language.name: Qwen3.5 27B Opus Distilled v2
language.summary: Improved v2 distillation of Qwen3.5 27B with ~24% shorter chain-of-thought and 31.6% more correct solutions per token. 96.91% HumanEval pass@1. Desktop only.

language.name: Qwopus3.5 27B v3
language.summary: Reasoning-enhanced Qwen3.5 27B with tool-calling RL and act-then-refine agentic workflows. 95.73% HumanEval Plus. Best for desktop setups with high memory.

language.name: Gemma 4 26B-A4B IT
language.summary: Mixture-of-Experts Gemma 4 with 25.2B total params but only 3.8B active per token. Flagship-quality vision and 256K context in a memory-efficient MoE package.

language.name: Gemma 4 26B-A4B Tuned
language.summary: Gemma 4 26B-A4B variant tuned for more direct replies. MoE architecture with only 4B active params. Vision support via dedicated mmproj.

language.name: Qwen3.5 27B
language.summary: Desktop-class Qwen3.5 model tuned for stronger reasoning and coding quality. Intended for higher-memory laptops and workstations.

language.name: GLM-4.7 Flash Opus Distilled
language.summary: Large desktop-focused GLM variant distilled toward stronger Claude-style reasoning and longer-form answers. Best suited to Macs and PCs with high memory budgets.

language.name: Gemma 4 31B IT
language.summary: Google's flagship Gemma 4 dense model. 30.7B params, 256K context, vision, and top-tier benchmarks (MMLU Pro 85.2%, AIME 89.2%). Desktop only.

language.name: Gemma 4 31B Tuned
language.summary: Gemma 4 31B variant tuned for more direct responses. Full dense 31B architecture for maximum quality. Desktop only.

language.name: Hermes 4.3 36B
language.summary: Latest flagship Hermes model based on ByteDance Seed 36B. 512K context window, best-in-class tool-calling, and strong reasoning. Desktop only.

language.name: Qwen3.5 35B-A3B
language.summary: Large Mixture-of-Experts Qwen3.5 option for high-end desktop setups where quality matters more than memory footprint.

language.name: Qwen3 Coder Next 80B-A3B
language.summary: MoE coding-specialist model with only 3B active parameters but 80B total. Excels at long-horizon reasoning, tool use, and agentic coding tasks. Desktop-only due to large memory footprint.

language.name: Qwen3.5 122B-A10B
language.summary: Ultra-large desktop MoE model for workstation-class hardware with very high memory capacity.

language.name: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B
language.summary: High-end Nemotron MoE option for workstation-class Macs and PCs. Expect long downloads, long load times, and a very large memory footprint.

language.name: Qwen3.5 REAP 262B-A17B
language.summary: Pruned Qwen3.5-397B variant using REAP (Router-weighted Expert Activation Pruning) to cut 35% of MoE experts. Near-397B quality in a smaller footprint, fits on a single 96 GB GPU with CPU offload.

language.name: Qwen3.5 397B-A17B
language.summary: Maximum-scale Qwen3.5 desktop option for enterprise-grade hardware and very large memory headroom.

image.name: Stable Diffusion 1.5
image.summary: Smallest image gen model. Fast on phones and desktops. Generates 512x512 images with good quality for its size.

image.name: SDXL Turbo
image.summary: Very fast image generation in just 4 steps. Good quality at 512x512. Great for quick iterations on newer phones and desktops.

image.name: Stable Diffusion 3.5 Medium
image.summary: Best quality-to-size ratio for image gen. Generates 1024x1024 images with strong prompt adherence and fine detail.

image.name: SDXL Lightning
image.summary: Lightning-fast SDXL distilled to 4 steps. Generates 1024x1024 images in under a second on desktop GPUs. Excellent quality for its speed.

image.name: Z-Image Turbo
image.summary: Blazing fast 6B model — 10x faster than FLUX with ~95% of the quality. Excellent bilingual text rendering (EN/CN). Only 3.7 GB. Apache 2.0.

image.name: SDXL Base 1.0
image.summary: Full-quality SDXL with 20-step generation. Produces detailed 1024x1024 images with strong prompt adherence. Desktop only.

image.name: Stable Diffusion 3.5 Large
image.summary: 8B-parameter MMDiT model. Major step up from SD 3.5 Medium with superior prompt understanding, typography, and fine detail at 1024x1024.

image.name: SD 3.5 Large Turbo
image.summary: Fast 4-step generation with 8B-parameter quality. Uses adversarial diffusion distillation for rapid high-quality output at 1024x1024.

image.name: Z-Image Base
image.summary: Full 6B model for maximum quality. Exceptional prompt adherence and photorealism at 1024x1024. Pairs with Qwen3-4B text encoder. Apache 2.0.

image.name: FLUX.1 Schnell
image.summary: State-of-the-art image generation in just 4 steps. Exceptional prompt understanding and photorealistic output. Desktop only — needs significant memory.

image.name: FLUX.1 Dev
image.summary: Highest-quality FLUX.1 model. 50-step generation produces exceptional photorealistic images with outstanding prompt fidelity. Non-commercial license.

image.name: Chroma1 HD Flash
image.summary: Fast distilled Chroma model. 8.9B FLUX-based architecture generating high-quality images in ~8 steps. Great speed-to-quality ratio. Apache 2.0.

image.name: Chroma1 HD
image.summary: 8.9B FLUX-based model tuned for HD image quality. Excellent prompt adherence, typography, and fine detail. 40-step generation at 1024x1024. Apache 2.0.

image.name: Ovis-Image 7B
image.summary: 7B model that punches above its weight — text rendering accuracy (0.92) beats 20B Qwen-Image (0.83) and GPT-4o (0.86). Built on FLUX architecture. Great for typography-heavy content.

image.name: FLUX.1 Schnell Q8
image.summary: Higher-fidelity quantization of FLUX.1 Schnell. Same blazing 4-step generation with noticeably sharper details. RAM is the bottleneck — 24 GB+ recommended.

image.name: FLUX.1 Dev Q8
image.summary: Premium quantization of FLUX.1 Dev. The best local image generation quality available — photorealistic with near-lossless detail. RAM is the bottleneck — 24 GB+ recommended. Non-commercial license.

image.name: Chroma1 HD Q8
image.summary: Premium quantization of Chroma1 HD. Near-lossless 8.9B FLUX-based model for the highest local image quality. RAM is the bottleneck — 24 GB+ recommended. Apache 2.0.

image.name: Chroma1 Radiance
image.summary: 10B pixel-space diffusion model — no VAE needed. Works directly on image pixels for exceptional detail and coherence. 40-step generation. Apache 2.0.

image.name: Qwen-Image
image.summary: 20B MMDiT — the text-to-image king. Dominates text rendering benchmarks (96.8 vs FLUX's 84.5). Best overall open-source image quality. Needs 24 GB+ RAM.

video.name: Wan 2.1 VACE 1.3B
video.summary: Versatile lightweight model supporting text-to-video, reference-to-video, and video-to-video. Same size as the base 1.3B with more capabilities.

video.name: Wan 2.1 T2V 1.3B
video.summary: Smallest video generation model. Generates short 2-second clips from text prompts. Fast enough for desktop, potentially viable on high-end phones.

video.name: Wan 2.2 TI2V 5B
video.summary: Mid-range text+image-to-video model. Good balance of quality and speed at 5B parameters. Uses Wan 2.2 VAE for improved decoding.

video.name: Wan 2.1 T2V 14B
video.summary: High-quality text-to-video generation. Produces detailed, coherent 2-4 second clips with strong prompt adherence. Desktop only — needs significant memory.

video.name: Wan 2.1 VACE 14B
video.summary: High-quality versatile model supporting text-to-video, reference-to-video, and video-to-video. More capable than the base 14B T2V model.

video.name: Wan 2.1 I2V 14B 480P
video.summary: Image-to-video model — animates a still image into a short video clip at 480p. Requires a CLIP vision encoder for image understanding.

video.name: Wan 2.1 I2V 14B 720P
video.summary: Image-to-video at 720p — animates a still image into a higher-resolution video. Same architecture as 480P variant but supports larger output.

video.name: Wan 2.1 FLF2V 14B 720P
video.summary: First-last-frame-to-video — provide a start and end image and the model generates the transition video between them. 720p output.

video.name: Wan 2.2 T2V A14B
video.summary: Highest-quality text-to-video. Dual-stage architecture uses separate low-noise and high-noise models for superior coherence and detail. Requires ~20 GB VRAM.

video.name: Wan 2.2 I2V A14B
video.summary: Highest-quality image-to-video. Dual-stage architecture animates still images with exceptional motion coherence. Requires ~20 GB VRAM.

voice.name: Whisper Tiny
voice.summary: Fastest STT, good for low-end devices. ~39 MB.

voice.name: Whisper Base
voice.summary: Balanced STT for most phones. ~74 MB.

voice.name: Whisper Medium
voice.summary: High accuracy STT for desktop. ~465 MB.

voice.name: Whisper Large v3 Turbo
voice.summary: Best accuracy STT. Near-human transcription. ~809 MB.

voice.name: Silero VAD v5
voice.summary: Voice activity detection. ~2 MB, bundled with the app.

voice.name: Kokoro TTS (English)
voice.summary: High-quality natural-sounding English TTS. Multiple voices. ~82 MB.

voice.name: Kokoro TTS (Multi-language)
voice.summary: Natural TTS supporting English, Chinese, and more. ~180 MB.

voice.name: Piper English (Amy)
voice.summary: Fast offline English TTS. Lightweight. ~20 MB.
